regimeSub: CPI 3.5% holds; Brent surges to $92 on Hormuz tensions while gold ATH $4125 signals risk hedging.
themes: June CPI 3.5% YoY vs May 4.2% peak validates NY Fed Williams 'inflation peaked' call (Jul 15) → 2y 4.21% +3bp, 10y 4.6% with curve steepening as market prices Warsh on hold through summer

Brent +$3.51 to $92.15 as OPEC+ Aug quota hike (Jul 6) collides with renewed Hormuz hostilities (Jul 13 CNBC) → Trump Iran sanctions push (Jul 19) keeps geopolitical bid under crude despite production growth
BOK hike to 2.75% (Jul 16) fully absorbed → USDKRW 1479 flat, KOSPI 7121 resilient despite Samsung/SK Hynix Kimi K3 selloff (Jul 20); TSMC $100bn US capex (Jul 16) anchors HBM supply crisis thesis

Gold +$98 to ATH $4125 on Iran sanction escalation risk (Trump Jul 19) + VIX compression 17.05 from 18.65 → classic haven bid without equity vol spike signals positioning for tail risk not imminent stress

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y real ~1.1% (4.6% nom - 3.5% CPI); positive real rates support USD but cap equity multiples

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: Curve +39bp (4.6% - 4.21%) steepening on term premium; inflation fears longer-dated vs Fed on hold

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained despite Brent surge; credit markets complacent on energy pass-through risk

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 17.05 down from 18.65; equity vol compressed despite $92 Brent and gold ATH divergence

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $92.15 +4% as Hormuz hostilities (Jul 13) offset OPEC+ quota hike; watching $95 resistance

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4125 ATH +2.4%; Trump Iran sanctions push (Jul 19) driving haven flows over USD

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1479 flat post-BOK hike; won at 2-month highs absorbing rate differential shift

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Kimi K3 shock (Jul 20) hit Samsung/SK but TSMC capex raise (Jul 16-17) backstops sector thesis

headline: Oil surges to $92 on Hormuz tensions and gold hits ATH $4125 as markets digest disinflation (CPI 3.5%) while Fed Chair Warsh holds rates steady.
